title: Backends: SB vs CRP
text: vignette compares two mixture backends: SB: Stick-breaking (finite truncation), controlled components CRP: Chinese Restaurant Process (cluster allocations)

title: Backends: SB vs CRP
text: backends represent DP mixture, differ random measure GG constructed. stick-breaking backend uses truncated stick-breaking representation, CRP backend uses allocation-based partitioning scheme. practice, SB offers explicit control truncation, CRP adapts number clusters allocation process.

title: Backends: SB vs CRP
code: library(DPmixGPD)  data("faithful", package = "datasets") y <- faithful$eruptions

title: Backends: SB vs CRP
code: bundle_sb <- build_nimble_bundle(   y = y,   backend = "sb",   kernel = "normal",   GPD = TRUE,   components = 6,   mcmc = mcmc )  fit_sb <- run_mcmc_bundle_manual(bundle_sb, show_progress = FALSE)

title: Backends: SB vs CRP
code: bundle_crp <- build_nimble_bundle(   y = y,   backend = "crp",   kernel = "normal",   GPD = TRUE,   components = 6,   mcmc = mcmc )  fit_crp <- run_mcmc_bundle_manual(bundle_crp, show_progress = FALSE)

title: Backends: SB vs CRP
text: Posterior Mean Comparison: SB vs CRP
code: mean_sb <- predict(fit_sb, type = "mean", cred.level = 0.90, interval = "credible")$fit mean_crp <- predict(fit_crp, type = "mean", cred.level = 0.90, interval = "credible")$fit  comparison_df <- data.frame(   Backend = c("SB", "CRP"),   Estimate = c(mean_sb$estimate[1], mean_crp$estimate[1]),   Lower = c(mean_sb$lower[1], mean_crp$lower[1]),   Upper = c(mean_sb$upper[1], mean_crp$upper[1]) )  kable(comparison_df, digits = 3, align = "c",       caption = "Posterior Mean Comparison: SB vs CRP") %>%   kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"),                 full_width = FALSE, position = "center")

title: Causal Workflow
text: vignette demonstrates causal inference workflow using build_causal_bundle() run_mcmc_causal(). package computes distributional treatment effects: Average Treatment Effect (ATE): E[Y(1)−Y(0)∣X]E[Y(1) - Y(0) \mid X] Quantile Treatment Effect (QTE): QY(1)(τ)−QY(0)(τ)Q_{Y(1)}(\tau) - Q_{Y(0)}(\tau)

title: Causal Workflow
text: causal workflow models potential outcome distributions treated control groups adjusting covariates (optionally) propensity scores. treatment arm $t \\\\{0,1\\}$, $$ f_t(y \\mid x) = \\int K(y; \\theta_t(x))\\, dG_t(\\theta_t), $$ causal estimands derived differences fitted distributions, $\\mathbb{E}[Y(1) - Y(0) \\mid X]$ quantile contrasts.

title: Causal Workflow
code: library(DPmixGPD)  data("mtcars", package = "datasets") df <- mtcars X <- df[, c("wt", "hp", "qsec", "cyl")] X <- as.data.frame(X) T_ind <- df$am y <- df$mpg

title: Causal Workflow
text: build_causal_bundle() function creates unified structure containing: Propensity score (PS) model (optional) Control arm outcome model Treated arm outcome model
code: bundle <- build_causal_bundle(   y = y,   X = X,   T = T_ind,   backend = "sb",   kernel = "normal",   GPD = TRUE,   components = 6,   PS = "logit",   design = "observational",   mcmc_outcome = mcmc,   mcmc_ps = mcmc )  bundle #> DPmixGPD causal bundle #> PS model: Bayesian logit (T | X)  #> Outcome (treated): backend = sb | kernel = normal  #> Outcome (control): backend = sb | kernel = normal  #> GPD tail (treated/control): TRUE / TRUE  #> components (treated/control): 6 / 6  #> Outcome PS included: TRUE  #> epsilon (treated/control): 0.025 / 0.025  #> n (control) = 19 | n (treated) = 13

title: Causal Workflow
code: fit <- run_mcmc_causal(bundle, show_progress = FALSE)

title: Causal Workflow
code: ate_result <- ate(fit, interval = "hpd", nsim_mean = 100) print(ate_result) #> ATE (Average Treatment Effect) #>   Prediction points: 32 #>   Conditional (covariates): YES #>   Propensity score used: YES #>   PS scale: logit #>   Posterior mean draws: 100 #>   Credible interval: hpd #>  #> ATE estimates (treated - control): #>  id      estimate         lower        upper #>   1  7.096668e+66 -2.747790e+27 7.637892e+39 #>   2  1.092792e+67 -4.880833e+27 7.560155e+39 #>   3  3.148231e+57 -7.112172e+25 5.379520e+33 #>   4  6.368702e+67 -3.863970e+28 7.036002e+39 #>   5 2.951968e+104 -1.131116e+40 3.774128e+63 #>   6  1.570600e+65 -1.172550e+28 1.350685e+38 #> ... (26 more rows)  X_new <- data.frame(   wt = seq(min(X$wt), max(X$wt), length.out = 20),   hp = stats::median(X$hp),   qsec = stats::median(X$qsec),   cyl = stats::median(X$cyl) ) ate_grid <- ate(fit, newdata = X_new, interval = "hpd", nsim_mean = 100, level = 0.90)  print(ate_grid) #> ATE (Average Treatment Effect) #>   Prediction points: 20 #>   Conditional (covariates): YES #>   Propensity score used: YES #>   PS scale: logit #>   Posterior mean draws: 100 #>   Credible interval: hpd #>  #> ATE estimates (treated - control): #>  id     estimate         lower        upper #>   1 3.562911e+74 -1.060663e+31 9.152400e+29 #>   2 3.155030e+74 -1.105315e+31 7.386341e+29 #>   3 4.198869e+74 -7.024421e+30 6.078306e+29 #>   4 4.386679e+74 -7.786960e+30 5.286629e+29 #>   5 4.331852e+74 -6.407762e+30 4.840438e+29 #>   6 4.791778e+74 -5.115586e+30 3.809410e+29 #> ... (14 more rows) summary(ate_grid) #> ATE Summary #> ==================================================  #> Prediction points: 20 #> Conditional: YES | PS used: YES #> Posterior mean draws: 100 #> Interval: hpd #>  #> Model specification: #>   Backend (trt/con): sb / sb #>   Kernel (trt/con): normal / normal #>   GPD tail (trt/con): YES / YES #>  #> ATE statistics: #>   Mean: 5.65186376147264e+74 | Median: 5.12898071566214e+74 #>   Range: [3.15502974835712e+74, 9.2349665402096e+74] #>   SD: 1.61418131241249e+74 #>  #> Credible interval width: #>   Mean: 4.47971558863383e+30 | Median: 3.75941826993285e+30 #>   Range: [2.08686307002747e+26, 1.17917838120348e+31]

title: Causal Workflow
text: plot() method ATE objects supports multiple visualization types: type = "" (default): Returns list trt_control treatment_effect plots type = "effect": Treatment effect curve credible intervals type = "arms": Treated vs control mean outcomes
code: ate_plots <- plot(ate_grid) ate_plots$treatment_effect ate_plots$trt_control

title: Causal Workflow
code: probs <- c(0.1, 0.5, 0.9) qte_result <- qte(fit, probs = probs, interval = "hpd") print(qte_result) #> QTE (Quantile Treatment Effect) #>   Prediction points: 32 #>   Quantile grid: 0.1, 0.5, 0.9 #>   Conditional (covariates): YES #>   Propensity score used: YES #>   PS scale: logit #>   Credible interval: hpd #>  #> QTE estimates (treated - control): #>  index id      estimate         lower        upper #>    0.1  1  6.504045e+65 -2.256136e+26 5.320135e+38 #>    0.1  2  1.037103e+66 -3.513881e+26 5.328788e+38 #>    0.1  3  3.980819e+56 -5.332544e+24 4.169594e+32 #>    0.1  4  6.539472e+66 -3.865800e+27 4.825881e+38 #>    0.1  5 3.301256e+103 -1.026751e+39 3.286743e+62 #>    0.1  6  1.636091e+64 -7.144390e+26 6.866406e+36 #> ... (90 more rows)  qte_grid <- qte(fit, probs = probs, newdata = X_new, interval = "hpd") print(qte_grid) #> QTE (Quantile Treatment Effect) #>   Prediction points: 20 #>   Quantile grid: 0.1, 0.5, 0.9 #>   Conditional (covariates): YES #>   Propensity score used: YES #>   PS scale: logit #>   Credible interval: hpd #>  #> QTE estimates (treated - control): #>  index id     estimate         lower        upper #>    0.1  1 3.701822e+73 -8.451444e+29 2.588857e+43 #>    0.1  2 3.881035e+73 -7.416909e+29 2.664172e+43 #>    0.1  3 4.068925e+73 -6.509011e+29 2.741678e+43 #>    0.1  4 4.265911e+73 -5.712248e+29 2.821439e+43 #>    0.1  5 4.472434e+73 -5.013017e+29 2.903521e+43 #>    0.1  6 4.688955e+73 -4.399377e+29 2.987990e+43 #> ... (54 more rows) summary(qte_grid) #> QTE Summary #> ==================================================  #> Prediction points: 20 | Quantiles: 3 #> Quantile grid: 0.1, 0.5, 0.9 #> Conditional: YES | PS used: YES #> Interval: hpd #>  #> Model specification: #>   Backend (trt/con): sb / sb #>   Kernel (trt/con): normal / normal #>   GPD tail (trt/con): YES / YES #>  #> QTE by quantile: #>  quantile     mean_qte   median_qte      min_qte      max_qte       sd_qte #>       0.1 6.017011e+73 5.802194e+73 3.701822e+73 9.073018e+73 1.668230e+73 #>       0.5 3.939864e+74 3.798390e+74 2.423385e+74 5.948655e+74 1.093560e+74 #>       0.9 1.291351e+75 1.244946e+75 7.942781e+74 1.950085e+75 3.584829e+74 #>  #> Credible interval width: #>   Mean: 4.54299494313147e+44 | Median: 2.19277425358873e+44 #>   Range: [7.05972174744674e+28, 1.53846697783865e+45]

title: Causal Workflow
text: plot() method QTE objects supports multiple visualization types: type = "" (default): Returns list trt_control treatment_effect plots type = "effect": Quantile treatment effect curves faceted quantile level type = "arms": Treated vs control quantile curves
code: qte_plots <- plot(qte_grid) qte_plots$treatment_effect qte_plots$trt_control

title: Causal Workflow
code: summary(fit) #> -- PS fit -- #> DPmixGPD PS fit #> model: logit  #>  #> -- Outcome fits -- #> [control] #> MixGPD fit | backend: Stick-Breaking Process | kernel: Normal Distribution | GPD tail: TRUE #> n = 19 | components = 6 | epsilon = 0.025 #> MCMC: niter=400, nburnin=100, thin=2, nchains=1  #> Fit #> Use summary() for posterior summaries; plot() for diagnostics; predict() for predictions. #>  #> [treated] #> MixGPD fit | backend: Stick-Breaking Process | kernel: Normal Distribution | GPD tail: TRUE #> n = 13 | components = 6 | epsilon = 0.025 #> MCMC: niter=400, nburnin=100, thin=2, nchains=1  #> Fit #> Use summary() for posterior summaries; plot() for diagnostics; predict() for predictions.

title: Conditional Models
text: vignette demonstrates fitting conditional model covariates generating predictions grid new covariate values.

title: Conditional Models
text: Conditional models allow bulk mixture parameters vary covariates. write conditional density $$ f(y_i \\mid x_i) = \\int K(y_i; \\theta(x_i))\\, dG(\\theta), $$ link $\\theta$ xix_i encoded kernel-specific regression structure. DP prior provides flexible, data-driven clustering local distributions across covariate space.

title: Conditional Models
code: library(DPmixGPD)  data("mtcars", package = "datasets") df <- mtcars y <- df$mpg X <- df[, c("wt", "hp")] X <- as.data.frame(X)

title: Conditional Models
code: bundle <- build_nimble_bundle(   y = y,   X = X,   backend = "sb",   kernel = "normal",   GPD = TRUE,   components = 6,   mcmc = mcmc )  fit <- run_mcmc_bundle_manual(bundle, show_progress = FALSE)

title: Conditional Models
code: q_levels <- c(0.1, 0.5, 0.9) q_fits <- lapply(q_levels, function(tau) {   predict(fit, x = new_X, type = "quantile", index = tau, cred.level = 0.90, interval = "credible")$fit })  q_df <- do.call(rbind, Map(function(tau, df) {   data.frame(wt = new_X$wt, tau = tau, estimate = df$estimate, lower = df$lower, upper = df$upper) }, q_levels, q_fits))  head(q_df) #>         wt tau   estimate      lower    upper #> 1 1.513000 0.1 -0.2925464 -3.6660604 1.934948 #> 2 1.675958 0.1  0.2137438 -3.0245694 2.076944 #> 3 1.838917 0.1  0.6850905 -2.2964308 2.237707 #> 4 2.001875 0.1  1.1297091 -1.5130531 2.418173 #> 5 2.164833 0.1  1.5285199 -0.9637076 2.620008 #> 6 2.327792 0.1  1.8950226 -0.4024057 2.830299 ggplot(q_df, aes(x = wt, y = estimate, color = factor(tau))) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper, fill = factor(tau)), alpha = 0.2, color = NA) +   labs(x = "Weight (wt)", y = "Predicted Quantile", color = "Quantile", fill = "Quantile",        title = "Conditional Quantile Curves at Median Horsepower") +   theme_minimal() +   theme(legend.position = "bottom")

title: Available Distributions
text: DPmixGPD supports multiple bulk kernels mixture components can optionally splice Generalized Pareto Distribution (GPD) tail beyond threshold. examples use vectorized R wrappers (lowercase) . d*, p*, q*, r* prefixes mean: d*: density (PDF); supports log available. p*: distribution (CDF); supports lower.tail log.p. q*: quantile; supports lower.tail log.p. r*: random generation; supports n. GPD variants add threshold, tail_scale, tail_shape. See Complete Function Reference end full d/p/q/r sets (R + NIMBLE) Rd links.

title: Available Distributions
text: Functions Base: dgpd, pgpd, qgpd, rgpd GPD parameter sets GPD function demo (x = 2.0, p = 0.8, n = 3)
code: sets <- list(   list(label = "GPD A", fn = dgpd, params = list(threshold = 1.0, scale = 0.6, shape = 0.15)),   list(label = "GPD B", fn = dgpd, params = list(threshold = 1.5, scale = 0.5, shape = 0.25)),   list(label = "GPD C", fn = dgpd, params = list(threshold = 2.0, scale = 0.7, shape = 0.10)) )  param_table("GPD parameter sets", sets) variants <- list(   list(label = "GPD A", d = dgpd, p = pgpd, q = qgpd, r = rgpd,        params = list(threshold = 1.0, scale = 0.6, shape = 0.15)),   list(label = "GPD B", d = dgpd, p = pgpd, q = qgpd, r = rgpd,        params = list(threshold = 1.5, scale = 0.5, shape = 0.25)),   list(label = "GPD C", d = dgpd, p = pgpd, q = qgpd, r = rgpd,        params = list(threshold = 2.0, scale = 0.7, shape = 0.10)) )  demo_table("GPD function demo (x = 2.0, p = 0.8, n = 3)", variants, x = 2.0)

title: Available Distributions
text: Functions Base: dnorm, pnorm, qnorm, rnorm Base + GPD: dnormgpd, pnormgpd, qnormgpd, rnormgpd Mixture: dnormmix, pnormmix, qnormmix, rnormmix Mixture + GPD: dnormmixgpd, pnormmixgpd, qnormmixgpd, rnormmixgpd Normal parameter sets Normal function demo (x = 0.5, p = 0.8, n = 3)
code: sets <- list(   list(label = "Base", fn = dnorm, params = list(mean = 0, sd = 1)),   list(label = "Base + GPD", fn = dnormgpd,        params = list(mean = 0, sd = 1, threshold = 1.5, tail_scale = 0.5, tail_shape = 0.2)),   list(label = "Mixture", fn = dnormmix,        params = list(w = c(0.7, 0.3), mean = c(-1, 1.5), sd = c(1.0, 0.7))),   list(label = "Mixture + GPD", fn = dnormmixgpd,        params = list(w = c(0.7, 0.3), mean = c(-1, 1), sd = c(1.0, 0.7),                      threshold = 1.5, tail_scale = 0.5, tail_shape = 0.2)) )  param_table("Normal parameter sets", sets) variants <- list(   list(label = "Base", d = dnorm, p = pnorm, q = qnorm, r = rnorm,        params = list(mean = 0, sd = 1)),   list(label = "Base + GPD", d = dnormgpd, p = pnormgpd, q = qnormgpd, r = rnormgpd,        params = list(mean = 0, sd = 1, threshold = 1.5, tail_scale = 0.5, tail_shape = 0.2)),   list(label = "Mixture", d = dnormmix, p = pnormmix, q = qnormmix, r = rnormmix,        params = list(w = c(0.7, 0.3), mean = c(-1, 1.5), sd = c(1.0, 0.7))),   list(label = "Mixture + GPD", d = dnormmixgpd, p = pnormmixgpd, q = qnormmixgpd, r = rnormmixgpd,        params = list(w = c(0.7, 0.3), mean = c(-1, 1), sd = c(1.0, 0.7),                      threshold = 1.5, tail_scale = 0.5, tail_shape = 0.2)) )  demo_table("Normal function demo (x = 0.5, p = 0.8, n = 3)", variants, x = 0.5)

title: Available Distributions
text: Functions Base: dgamma, pgamma, qgamma, rgamma Base + GPD: dgammagpd, pgammagpd, qgammagpd, rgammagpd Mixture: dgammamix, pgammamix, qgammamix, rgammamix Mixture + GPD: dgammamixgpd, pgammamixgpd, qgammamixgpd, rgammamixgpd Gamma parameter sets Gamma function demo (x = 3.0, p = 0.8, n = 3)
code: sets <- list(   list(label = "Base", fn = dgamma, params = list(shape = 2.5, scale = 1.0)),   list(label = "Base + GPD", fn = dgammagpd,        params = list(shape = 3, scale = 1.0, threshold = 5.0, tail_scale = 1.0, tail_shape = 0.2)),   list(label = "Mixture", fn = dgammamix,        params = list(w = c(0.6, 0.4), shape = c(2, 5), scale = c(1.0, 0.7))),   list(label = "Mixture + GPD", fn = dgammamixgpd,        params = list(w = c(0.6, 0.4), shape = c(2, 5), scale = c(1.0, 0.7),                      threshold = 5.0, tail_scale = 1.0, tail_shape = 0.2)) )  param_table("Gamma parameter sets", sets) variants <- list(   list(label = "Base", d = dgamma, p = pgamma, q = qgamma, r = rgamma,        params = list(shape = 2.5, scale = 1.0)),   list(label = "Base + GPD", d = dgammagpd, p = pgammagpd, q = qgammagpd, r = rgammagpd,        params = list(shape = 3, scale = 1.0, threshold = 5.0, tail_scale = 1.0, tail_shape = 0.2)),   list(label = "Mixture", d = dgammamix, p = pgammamix, q = qgammamix, r = rgammamix,        params = list(w = c(0.6, 0.4), shape = c(2, 5), scale = c(1.0, 0.7))),   list(label = "Mixture + GPD", d = dgammamixgpd, p = pgammamixgpd, q = qgammamixgpd, r = rgammamixgpd,        params = list(w = c(0.6, 0.4), shape = c(2, 5), scale = c(1.0, 0.7),                      threshold = 5.0, tail_scale = 1.0, tail_shape = 0.2)) )  demo_table("Gamma function demo (x = 3.0, p = 0.8, n = 3)", variants, x = 3.0)

title: Available Distributions
text: Functions Base: dlnorm, plnorm, qlnorm, rlnorm Base + GPD: dlognormalgpd, plognormalgpd, qlognormalgpd, rlognormalgpd Mixture: dlognormalmix, plognormalmix, qlognormalmix, rlognormalmix Mixture + GPD: dlognormalmixgpd, plognormalmixgpd, qlognormalmixgpd, rlognormalmixgpd Lognormal parameter sets Lognormal function demo (x = 1.2, p = 0.8, n = 3)

title: Available Distributions
text: Complete distribution reference

title: Available Distributions
text: NIMBLE (CamelCase): scalar functions intended model code. R (lowercase): vectorized wrappers intended analysis plotting. parameter names used; R versions vectorize first argument.

title: DPmixGPD: Quick Start
text: vignette provides end--end introduction DPmixGPD workflow: Build model specification Run MCMC sampling Extract fitted values predictions

title: DPmixGPD: Quick Start
text: DPmixGPD models bulk distribution Dirichlet process (DP) mixture, optionally splices Generalized Pareto Distribution (GPD) tail beyond threshold uu. outcomes yiy_i kernel KK, bulk model $$ f(y_i) = \\int K(y_i; \\theta)\\, dG(\\theta), \\quad G \\sim \\mathrm{DP}(\\alpha, G_0). $$ tail included, density replaced y>uy > u GPD tail scale shape parameters, preserving continuity uu.

title: DPmixGPD: Quick Start
text: DPmixGPD fits flexible mixture models bulk distribution can splice Generalized Pareto Distribution (GPD) tail beyond threshold. approach appropriate : center data well described single parametric family extreme right tail requires principled extrapolation

title: DPmixGPD: Quick Start
code: library(DPmixGPD)  data("faithful", package = "datasets") y <- faithful$eruptions  bundle <- build_nimble_bundle(   y = y,   backend = "sb",   kernel = "normal",   GPD = TRUE,   components = 6,   mcmc = mcmc )  fit <- run_mcmc_bundle_manual(bundle, show_progress = FALSE)

title: DPmixGPD: Quick Start
code: pred_mean <- predict(fit, type = "mean", cred.level = 0.90, interval = "credible") pred_q90  <- predict(fit, type = "quantile", index = 0.90, cred.level = 0.90, interval = "credible")  pred_mean$fit #>   estimate lower upper #> 1     3.19  3.05  3.32 pred_q90$fit #>   estimate index lower upper #> 1     4.55   0.9  4.45  4.67

title: Kernel reference: Amoroso
text: Amoroso distribution flexible positive-support family generalizes gamma Weibull-type shapes. parameters control location, scale, tail behavior, making useful standard parametric kernels rigid.

title: Kernel reference: Cauchy
text: Cauchy density heavy-tailed: $$ f(y) = \\frac{1}{\\pi\\gamma\\left[1 + \\left(\\frac{y-x_0}{\\gamma}\\right)^2\\right]}, $$ location x0x_0 scale $\\gamma$.

title: Kernel reference: Gamma
text: shape kk rate $\\beta$, gamma density y>0y>0 $$ f(y) = \\frac{\\beta^k}{\\Gamma(k)} y^{k-1} e^{-\\beta y}. $$

title: Kernel reference: Inverse Gaussian
text: inverse Gaussian positive-support distribution density $$ f(y) = \\left(\\frac{\\lambda}{2\\pi y^3}\\right)^{1/2} \\exp\\left(-\\frac{\\lambda (y-\\mu)^2}{2\\mu^2 y}\\right), $$ $\\mu$ mean $\\lambda$ shape parameter.

title: Kernel reference: Laplace
text: Laplace (double-exponential) density $$ f(y) = \\frac{1}{2b}\\exp\\left(-\\frac{|y-\\mu|}{b}\\right), $$ location $\\mu$ scale bb.

title: Kernel reference: Lognormal
text: $Z \\sim \\mathcal{N}(\\mu, \\sigma^2)$ $Y = \\exp(Z)$ lognormal. kernel density y>0y>0: $$ f(y) = \\frac{1}{y\\sigma\\sqrt{2\\pi}}\\exp\\left(-\\frac{(\\log y-\\mu)^2}{2\\sigma^2}\\right). $$

title: Kernels Overview
text: DPmixGPD supports multiple mixture kernel families flexible density estimation. kernel different properties suited various data types modeling scenarios.

title: Kernels Overview
text: kernels location scale parameters, making suitable real-valued data: Normal - Gaussian kernel mean sd parameters. Best symmetric, bell-shaped distributions. Cauchy - Heavy-tailed kernel location scale parameters. Robust outliers support GPD tail. Laplace - Double exponential kernel location scale parameters. heavier tails Normal.

title: Kernels Overview
text: kernels defined positive values , ideal positive data like durations, sizes, amounts: Lognormal - Log-transformed Normal meanlog sdlog parameters. Good right-skewed positive data. Gamma - Flexible positive kernel shape scale parameters. Can model various skewness levels. Inverse Gaussian - Alternative positive kernel mean shape parameters. Often used waiting times.

title: Kernels Overview
text: Amoroso - Four-parameter generalization loc, scale, shape1, shape2. Includes Gamma, Weibull, distributions special cases.

title: Kernels Overview
text: kernels support GPD tail modeling (GPD = TRUE):

title: Kernels Overview

title: 1. Introduction to DPmixGPD
text: DP mixtures represent densities $f(y)=\\int K(y;\\theta)\\,dG(\\theta)$ $G \\sim \\mathrm{DP}(\\alpha, G_0)$. Optional GPD splicing replaces tail beyond threshold, providing principled extreme-value behavior.

title: 1. Introduction to DPmixGPD
text: DPmixGPD R package Bayesian nonparametric Dirichlet Process mixture models flexible kernels optional Generalized Pareto Distribution (GPD) tail augmentation.

previous_headings: DPmixGPD: Bayesian Nonparametric Mixture Models with GPD Tails > Overview
title: 1. Introduction to DPmixGPD
text: Flexible mixture kernels: normal, gamma, lognormal, laplace, invgauss, amoroso, cauchy Two MCMC backends: Stick-Breaking (SB) Chinese Restaurant Process (CRP) Optional GPD tail: Enhance tail probability estimation Conditional modeling: Add covariates via parameter linking Causal workflows: Propensity score adjustment multiple PS strategies Comprehensive prediction: density, survival, quantile, sample, mean

previous_headings: DPmixGPD: Bayesian Nonparametric Mixture Models with GPD Tails > Model Structure
title: 1. Introduction to DPmixGPD
text: Bulk: Dirichlet Process mixture Yi∼∑k=1∞wkfk(yi;θk)Y_i \sim \sum_{k=1}^{\infty} w_k f_k(y_i; \theta_k) Tail (optional): GPD beyond threshold P(Y>u|Y>u)∼GPD(scale,shape)P(Y > u | Y > u) \sim \text{GPD}(\text{scale}, \text{shape})

previous_headings: DPmixGPD: Bayesian Nonparametric Mixture Models with GPD Tails > Model Structure
title: 1. Introduction to DPmixGPD
text: Two outcome arms optional propensity score (PS) adjustment: - Control (T=0T=0): Yi(0)∼DPMcon(Xi,PSi)Y_i^{(0)} \sim \text{DPM}_{\text{con}}(X_i, PS_i) - Treated (T=1T=1): Yi(1)∼DPMtrt(Xi,PSi)Y_i^{(1)} \sim \text{DPM}_{\text{trt}}(X_i, PS_i)

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
text: vignette documents probability distributions exported DPmixGPD corresponding d/p/q/r functions used throughout package. continuous random variable YY parameter scalars 𝛉\boldsymbol{\theta}, use standard quartet: Density (PDF): f(y∣𝛉)f(y\mid\boldsymbol{\theta}), implemented d*(). Distribution (CDF): F(y∣𝛉)=Pr(Y≤y)F(y\mid\boldsymbol{\theta})=\Pr(Y\le y), implemented p*(). Quantile: Q(p∣𝛉)=inf{y:F(y∣𝛉)≥p}Q(p\mid\boldsymbol{\theta}) = \inf\{y: F(y\mid\boldsymbol{\theta})\ge p\}, implemented q*(). Random generation: Y=Q(U∣𝛉)Y = Q(U\mid\boldsymbol{\theta}) U∼Unif(0,1)U\sim \text{Unif}(0,1), implemented r*(). d*() functions optionally return log-densities via log = TRUE, .e., logf(y∣𝛉)\log f(y\mid\boldsymbol{\theta}). numerically convenient likelihoods multiply densities add log-densities: log{∏=1nf(yi∣𝛉)}=∑=1nlogf(yi∣𝛉). \log\Big\{\prod_{=1}^n f(y_i\mid\boldsymbol{\theta})\Big\}=\sum_{=1}^n \log f(y_i\mid\boldsymbol{\theta}). p*() functions support lower.tail log.p: Pr(Y>y)=1−F(y),logF(y)orlog{1−F(y)}. \Pr(Y>y)=1-F(y),\qquad \log F(y)\ \text{}\ \log\{1-F(y)\}. lowercase d*(), p*(), q*() functions (e.g., dgammamix, pgammamix) accept vector inputs first argument evaluate elementwise; r*() supports n > 1 (including n = 0). CamelCase versions (e.g., dGammaMix) NIMBLE-compatible scalar functions use model code.
code: w <- c(0.60, 0.40) shape <- c(2.0, 5.0) scale <- c(1.0, 2.0)  # Use lowercase vectorized wrappers for R-side usage dgammamix(seq(0.5, 2.0, length.out = 5), w = w, shape = shape, scale = scale) [1] 0.182 0.219 0.216 0.194 0.165 qgammamix(c(0.1, 0.5, 0.9), w = w, shape = shape, scale = scale) [1]  0.731  3.126 12.550 rgammamix(5, w = w, shape = shape, scale = scale) [1]  1.127  3.463 10.452  0.578  8.976

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
text: Several kernels available finite mixtures. Let fj(⋅∣𝛉j)f_j(\cdot\mid\boldsymbol{\theta}_j) denote jjth component density let 𝐰=(w1,…,wJ)\boldsymbol{w}=(w_1,\ldots,w_J) mixture weights wj≥0w_j\ge 0 ∑j=1Jwj=1\sum_{j=1}^J w_j=1. mixture density CDF f(y)=∑j=1Jwjfj(y∣𝛉j),F(y)=∑j=1JwjFj(y∣𝛉j). f(y)=\sum_{j=1}^J w_j f_j(y\mid\boldsymbol{\theta}_j), \qquad F(y)=\sum_{j=1}^J w_j F_j(y\mid\boldsymbol{\theta}_j). Random generation proceeds sampling latent component index Z∼Categorical(𝐰)Z\sim\text{Categorical}(\boldsymbol{w}), sampling Y∣Z=j∼fj(⋅∣𝛉j)Y\mid Z=j\sim f_j(\cdot\mid\boldsymbol{\theta}_j). Mixture quantiles typically require numerical inversion mixture CDF.

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
text: Generalized Pareto Distribution (GPD) used tail modeling relative threshold uu. spliced kernels (*Gpd functions), bulk kernel used uu GPD tail attached uu, parameters (u,σ,ξ)(u,\sigma,\xi). code, represented threshold = u, tail_scale = \sigma, tail_shape = \xi (standalone GPD functions use scale shape σ\sigma ξ\xi).

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
text: standalone GPD functions dGpd, pGpd, qGpd, rGpd implement distribution XXabove threshold uu. x≥ux\ge u, density f(x;u,σ,ξ)=1σ(1+ξx−uσ)−1/ξ−1,σ>0,1+ξx−uσ>0. f(x;u,\sigma,\xi)=\frac{1}{\sigma}\left(1+\xi\frac{x-u}{\sigma}\right)^{-1/\xi-1}, \quad \sigma>0, \quad 1+\xi\frac{x-u}{\sigma}>0. CDF F(x)=1−(1+ξx−uσ)−1/ξ,x≥u, F(x)=1-\left(1+\xi\frac{x-u}{\sigma}\right)^{-1/\xi}, \quad x\ge u,  quantile Q(p)=u+σξ[(1−p)−ξ−1],p∈(0,1),ξ≠0, Q(p)=u+\frac{\sigma}{\xi}\Big[(1-p)^{-\xi}-1\Big],\quad p\(0,1),\ \xi\ne 0,  exponential-limit case obtained ξ→0\xi\0. Parameter mapping (math →\rightarrow code): u→u\tothreshold, σ→\sigma\toscale, ξ→\xi\toshape. Log-densities returned log = TRUE. show DPmixGPD kernels pairs: bulk-GPD-augmented. GPD tail threshold uu uses fGPD(x;u,σ,ξ)=1σ(1+ξx−uσ)−1/ξ−1for x≥u.f_{GPD}(x; u, \sigma, \xi) = \frac{1}{\sigma} \left(1 + \xi \frac{x-u}{\sigma}\right)^{-1/\xi - 1} \ \text{} x \ge u. standalone GPD tail distribution available via dGpd, pGpd, qGpd, rGpd functions.  subsection defines available d/p/q/rd/p/q/r functions, prints example outputs fixed parameters, overlays density curves three parameter sets clear legends. parameter sets reused within bulk GPD variants section.

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
text: normal component parameterized μ∈ℝ\mu\\mathbb{R} σ>0\sigma>0: f(y∣μ,σ)=12πσexp(−(y−μ)22σ2). f(y\mid\mu,\sigma)=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2\pi}\sigma}\exp\!\left(-\frac{(y-\mu)^2}{2\sigma^2}\right). finite normal mixture JJ components density f(y)=∑j=1Jwj𝒩(y∣μj,σj2),∑j=1Jwj=1. f(y)=\sum_{j=1}^J w_j\,\mathcal{N}(y\mid\mu_j,\sigma_j^2), \qquad \sum_{j=1}^J w_j=1. Parameter mapping (math →\rightarrow code): μj→\mu_j\tomean[j], σj→\sigma_j\tosd[j], wj→w_j\tow[j].

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
text: spliced version, bulk distribution normal mixture uu, GPD tail attached uu. tail parameters σ>0\sigma>0 ξ\xi, applied exceedances x−ux-u. Tail mapping (math →\rightarrow code): u→u\tothreshold, σ→\sigma\totail_scale, ξ→\xi\totail_shape.

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
text: gamma component shape α>0\alpha>0 scale β>0\beta>0 density f(y∣α,β)=1Γ(α)βαyα−1exp(−yβ),y>0. f(y\mid \alpha,\beta) = \frac{1}{\Gamma(\alpha)\,\beta^\alpha}\,y^{\alpha-1}\exp\!\left(-\frac{y}{\beta}\right), \qquad y>0. finite gamma mixture JJ components f(y)=∑j=1Jwjfj(y∣αj,βj),wj≥0,∑j=1Jwj=1. f(y)=\sum_{j=1}^J w_j\,f_j(y\mid \alpha_j,\beta_j), \qquad w_j\ge 0,\ \sum_{j=1}^J w_j=1. Parameter mapping (math →\rightarrow code): α→\alpha\toshape, β→\beta\toscale, weights wj→w_j\tow[j]. *MixGpd variant uses splicing idea: gamma mixture uu GPD tail uu. Tail mapping (math →\rightarrow code): u→u\tothreshold, σ→\sigma\totail_scale, ξ→\xi\totail_shape.

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
text: lognormal component defined logY∼𝒩(μ,σ2)\log Y \sim \mathcal{N}(\mu,\sigma^2), .e., f(y∣μ,σ)=1yσ2πexp(−(logy−μ)22σ2),y>0. f(y\mid\mu,\sigma)=\frac{1}{y\,\sigma\sqrt{2\pi}}\exp\!\left(-\frac{(\log y-\mu)^2}{2\sigma^2}\right),\quad y>0. finite lognormal mixture density f(y)=∑j=1JwjLognormal(y∣μj,σj2),y>0. f(y)=\sum_{j=1}^J w_j\,\text{Lognormal}(y\mid\mu_j,\sigma_j^2),\quad y>0. Parameter mapping (math →\rightarrow code): μj→\mu_j\tomeanlog[j], σj→\sigma_j\tosdlog[j], wj→w_j\tow[j].

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
text: *MixGpd variant uses splicing idea: lognormal mixture uu, GPD tail uu. Tail mapping (math →\rightarrow code): u→u\tothreshold, σ→\sigma\totail_scale, ξ→\xi\totail_shape.

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
text: inverse Gaussian component mean μ>0\mu>0 shape λ>0\lambda>0 density f(y∣μ,λ)=(λ2πy3)1/2exp(−λ(y−μ)22μ2y),y>0. f(y\mid \mu,\lambda) = \left(\frac{\lambda}{2\pi y^3}\right)^{1/2} \exp\!\left(-\frac{\lambda (y-\mu)^2}{2\mu^2 y}\right), \quad y>0. finite inverse Gaussian mixture f(y)=∑j=1JwjIG(y∣μj,λj),y>0. f(y)=\sum_{j=1}^J w_j\,\text{IG}(y\mid \mu_j,\lambda_j),\quad y>0. Parameter mapping (math →\rightarrow code): μj→\mu_j\tomean[j], λj→\lambda_j\toshape[j], wj→w_j\tow[j].

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
text: Amoroso component location ∈ℝa\\mathbb{R}, scale θ≠0\theta\neq 0, shapes α>0\alpha>0, β>0\beta>0 density (y>ay>) f(y∣,θ,α,β)=1Γ(α)|βθ|(y−aθ)αβ−1exp[−(y−aθ)β]. f(y\mid ,\theta,\alpha,\beta) = \frac{1}{\Gamma(\alpha)} \left|\frac{\beta}{\theta}\right| \left(\frac{y-}{\theta}\right)^{\alpha\beta-1} \exp\!\left[-\left(\frac{y-}{\theta}\right)^{\beta}\right]. finite Amoroso mixture f(y)=∑j=1Jwjfj(y∣aj,θj,αj,βj). f(y)=\sum_{j=1}^J w_j\,f_j(y\mid a_j,\theta_j,\alpha_j,\beta_j). Parameter mapping (math →\rightarrow code): aj→a_j\toloc[j], θj→\theta_j\toscale[j], αj→\alpha_j\toshape1[j], βj→\beta_j\toshape2[j], wj→w_j\tow[j].

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
text: section documents inverse Gaussian density , single component rather mixture: f(y∣μ,λ)=(λ2πy3)1/2exp(−λ(y−μ)22μ2y),y>0. f(y\mid \mu,\lambda) = \left(\frac{\lambda}{2\pi y^3}\right)^{1/2} \exp\!\left(-\frac{\lambda (y-\mu)^2}{2\mu^2 y}\right), \quad y>0. Parameter mapping (math →\rightarrow code): μ→\mu\tomean, λ→\lambda\toshape.

what: Inverse Gaussian base with GPD tail
title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
text: dInvGaussGpd, pInvGaussGpd, qInvGaussGpd, rInvGaussGpd functions splice base inverse Gaussian uu GPD tail uu. Tail mapping (math →\rightarrow code): u→u\tothreshold, σ→\sigma\totail_scale, ξ→\xi\totail_shape.

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
text: Cauchy distribution location x0∈ℝx_0\\mathbb{R} scale γ>0\gamma>0 density f(y∣x0,γ)=1πγ[1+(y−x0γ)2],y∈ℝ. f(y\mid x_0,\gamma)=\frac{1}{\pi\gamma\left[1+\left(\frac{y-x_0}{\gamma}\right)^2\right]}, \quad y\\mathbb{R}. finite Cauchy mixture f(y)=∑j=1JwjCauchy(y∣x0j,γj). f(y)=\sum_{j=1}^J w_j\,\text{Cauchy}(y\mid x_{0j},\gamma_j). Parameter mapping (math →\rightarrow code): x0→x_0\tolocation, γ→\gamma\toscale; mixtures x0j→x_{0j}\tolocation[j], γj→\gamma_j\toscale[j], wj→w_j\tow[j].

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
text: section provides compact reference distributions supported DPmixGPD corresponding user-facing function families. CRP backend uses base (single-kernel) functions, SB backend uses mixture (multi-component) functions. “Type” column indicates whether backend expects scalar parameters (single value per parameter) vectors indexed mixture component.

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
text: CRP Backend uses base (single-kernel) functions; parameters scalar. SB Backend uses mixture/spliced mixture functions; component-specific parameters typically vectors indexed component jj; mixture weights w required vector. GPD-, SB entries NA mixture-GPD wrapper SB family.

title: 3. Basic Workflow: Model Specification, Bundle, & MCMC
text: workflow builds DP mixture model, compiles NIMBLE code, explores posterior MCMC. Posterior draws used summarize density, quantiles, uncertainty model parameters.

title: 3. Basic Workflow: Model Specification, Bundle, & MCMC
text: DPmixGPD uses direct two-step workflow: Bundle (build_nimble_bundle): Generate NIMBLE code compile sampler. MCMC (run_mcmc_bundle_manual): Execute posterior sampling.

title: 3. Basic Workflow: Model Specification, Bundle, & MCMC
text: Purpose: Generate NIMBLE model code, compile sampler, prepare MCMC execution.

title: 4. Backends, Kernels, and Workflow Map
text: CRP stick-breaking backends two equivalent representations DP mixture. differ computation (allocation vs truncation) target underlying prior distributions.

title: 4. Backends, Kernels, and Workflow Map
text: DPmixGPD two orthogonal dials turn building models: CRP: Chinese Restaurant Process representation. SB: stick-breaking truncation fixed number components. Examples: normal, lognormal, gamma, inverse-Gaussian, Laplace, Cauchy, Amoroso, etc. Optionally, can also turn : GPD = TRUE/FALSE splice Generalized Pareto tail beyond threshold.

what: What changes between CRP and SB?
title: 4. Backends, Kernels, and Workflow Map
text: backends target posterior densities. difference representation: CRP learns random number occupied clusters within finite components cap. SB uses finite components cap learns stick-breaking weights. Practical rule thumb: CRP convenient want adaptive complexity still using finite components cap. SB convenient want predictable memory/time easy vectorization.

what: What does the workflow look like?
title: 4. Backends, Kernels, and Workflow Map
text: DPmixGPD uses consistent build -> run -> summarize loop: Build bundle using build_nimble_bundle() (causal builders TE work). Run MCMC using run_mcmc_bundle_manual(). Inspect summarize using print(), summary(), plot(). Predict using predict() (optionally fitted()).
code: # Build bundle <- build_nimble_bundle(   y = rnorm(50),   backend = "crp",   kernel = "normal",   GPD = FALSE,   components = 5,   mcmc = mcmc )  # Run fit <- load_or_fit("v04-backends-and-workflow-fit", run_mcmc_bundle_manual(bundle))  # Summarize print(fit) MixGPD fit | backend: Chinese Restaurant Process | kernel: Normal Distribution | GPD tail: FALSE n = 50 | components = 5 | epsilon = 0.025 MCMC: niter=400, nburnin=100, thin=2, nchains=1  Fit Use summary() for posterior summaries; plot() for diagnostics; predict() for predictions. summary(fit) MixGPD summary | backend: Chinese Restaurant Process | kernel: Normal Distribution | GPD tail: FALSE | epsilon: 0.025 n = 50 | components = 5 Summary Initial components: 5 | Components after truncation: 1  WAIC: 126.441 lppd: -57.216 | pWAIC: 6.004  Summary table   parameter  mean    sd q0.025 q0.500 q0.975    ess  weights[1] 0.962  0.08  0.735      1      1 23.073       alpha 0.306 0.291  0.005  0.218  1.046 46.898     mean[1] 0.128 0.133 -0.104  0.112  0.404    150       sd[1] 1.539 0.422  0.967  1.483  2.602 80.825 plot(fit) === histogram === === density === === traceplot === === running === === compare_partial === === autocorrelation === === geweke === === caterpillar === === pairs ===

title: 5. Unconditional DPmix (CRP Backend)
text: unconditional DP mixture models exchangeable outcomes via $f(y)=\\int K(y;\\theta)\\,dG(\\theta)$ $G \\sim \\mathrm{DP}(\\alpha, G_0)$. CRP backend implements using cluster allocations.

title: 5. Unconditional DPmix (CRP Backend)
text: vignette demonstrates unconditional DPmix model using CRP backend positive dataset. use Laplace kernel bulk distribution GPD tail augmentation.

title: 6. Unconditional DPmix with CRP Backend
text: unconditional DP mixture models exchangeable outcomes via $f(y)=\\int K(y;\\theta)\\,dG(\\theta)$ $G \\sim \\mathrm{DP}(\\alpha, G_0)$. CRP backend implements using cluster allocations.

title: 6. Unconditional DPmix with CRP Backend
text: Goal: Estimate density univariate outcome yy using nonparametric Dirichlet Process mixture Chinese Restaurant Process backend. Model: yi|G∼∫K(yi;θ)dG(θ)y_i | G \sim \int K(y_i; \theta) dG(\theta) G∼DP(α,G0)G \sim \text{DP}(\alpha, G_0) Backend: CRP truncation max components

title: 7. Unconditional DPmix with Stick-Breaking Backend
text: SB Backend: Fixed components keeps label-switching manageable, diagnostic plots via S3 plot() method show weight dynamics. Kernels matter: Gamma vs Cauchy can behave differently shoulders/tails, even without GPD tail. Predictions: Posterior density, posterior-mean quantiles, mean (via predictive sampling) accessible via predict() credible bands. Backend comparison: CRP fit (v04) SB-gamma fit deliver similar central quantiles SB offers control truncation. Next: Explore tail augmentation (GPD = TRUE) CRP backend v06 SB backend v07.

code: bundle_bulk_only <- build_nimble_bundle(   y = y_tail,   kernel = "lognormal",   backend = "crp",   GPD = FALSE,   components = 5,   mcmc = mcmc ) fit_bulk_only <- load_or_fit("v08-unconditional-DPmixGPD-CRP-fit_bulk_only", run_mcmc_bundle_manual(bundle_bulk_only)) bulk_quantiles <- predict(fit_bulk_only, type = "quantile", index = quantile_probs) tail_quantiles <- predict(fit_gpd, type = "quantile", index = quantile_probs)  compare_tbl <- bind_rows(   bulk_quantiles$fit %>% mutate(model = "Bulk only"),   tail_quantiles$fit %>% mutate(model = "Bulk + GPD") ) %>%   select(any_of(c("model", "index", "estimate", "lwr", "upr", "lower", "upper")))  compare_tbl %>%   mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 3))) %>%   kable(caption = "Posterior-Mean Quantiles for Bulk-Only vs GPD Models", align = "c") %>%   kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE, position = "center") plot(bulk_quantiles) plot(tail_quantiles)

title: 8. Unconditional DPmixGPD with CRP Backend
text: Threshold Sensitivity: 99th Quantile
code: sensitivity_fits <- lapply(thresholds, function(u) {   bundle <- build_nimble_bundle(     y = y_tail,     kernel = "invgauss",     backend = "crp",     GPD = TRUE,     param_specs = list(       gpd = list(         threshold = list(mode = "fixed", value = u)       )     ),     components = 5,     mcmc = mcmc   )   fit <- load_or_fit("v08-unconditional-DPmixGPD-CRP-fit", run_mcmc_bundle_manual(bundle))   list(threshold = u, fit = fit) })  sensitivity_tbl <- bind_rows(lapply(sensitivity_fits, function(entry) {   quant <- predict(entry$fit, type = "quantile", index = 0.99, interval = "credible")   qfit <- as.data.frame(quant$fit)   qfit$threshold <- entry$threshold    # Keep the usual columns if present   keep <- intersect(names(qfit), c("threshold", "index", "estimate", "lower", "upper", "lwr", "upr"))   qfit <- qfit[, keep, drop = FALSE]    # Standardize to one set of CI column names   if (!"lower" %in% names(qfit) && "lwr" %in% names(qfit)) qfit$lower <- qfit$lwr   if (!"upper" %in% names(qfit) && "upr" %in% names(qfit)) qfit$upper <- qfit$upr    # If estimate isn't present for some reason, fall back to the first numeric column   if (!"estimate" %in% names(qfit)) {     num_cols <- names(qfit)[vapply(qfit, is.numeric, logical(1))]     if (length(num_cols) > 0) qfit$estimate <- qfit[[num_cols[1]]]   }    out <- data.frame(     threshold = qfit$threshold[1],     q_99 = qfit$estimate[1],     q_99_lwr = if ("lower" %in% names(qfit)) qfit$lower[1] else NA_real_,     q_99_upr = if ("upper" %in% names(qfit)) qfit$upper[1] else NA_real_   )   tibble::as_tibble(out) }))  sensitivity_tbl %>%   mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 3))) %>%   kable(caption = "Threshold Sensitivity: 99th Quantile", align = "c") %>%   kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE, position = "center")

code: X_new <- expand.grid(   x1 = seq(-2, 2, length.out = 3),   x2 = c(0, 1),   x3 = 0 ) colnames(X_new) <- colnames(X)  y_grid <- seq(-1, 10, length.out = 200) densities_normal <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(X_new)), function(i) {   pred <- predict(fit_cond_normal, x = as.matrix(X_new[i, , drop = FALSE]), y = y_grid, type = "density")   data.frame(     y = pred$fit$y,     density = pred$fit$density,     group = paste0("x1=", round(X_new[i, "x1"], 1), ", x2=", X_new[i, "x2"]),     model = "Normal"   ) })  densities_amoroso <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(X_new)), function(i) {   pred <- predict(fit_cond_amoroso, x = as.matrix(X_new[i, , drop = FALSE]), y = y_grid, type = "density")   data.frame(     y = pred$fit$y,     density = pred$fit$density,     group = paste0("x1=", round(X_new[i, "x1"], 1), ", x2=", X_new[i, "x2"]),     model = "Amoroso (shape1=1)"   ) })  df_dens <- bind_rows(densities_normal, densities_amoroso)  ggplot(df_dens, aes(x = y, y = density, color = group)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   facet_wrap(~ model) +   labs(title = "Conditional Predictive Densities", x = "y", y = "Density") +   theme_minimal() +   theme(legend.position = "bottom")

code: X_new <- expand.grid(x1 = seq(-2, 2, length.out = 3), x2 = c(-1, 1), x3 = 0) colnames(X_new) <- colnames(X) y_min <- max(min(y), .Machine$double.eps) y_grid <- seq(y_min, max(y) * 1.1, length.out = 200)  densities_normal <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(X_new)), function(i) {   pred <- predict(fit_sb_normal, x = as.matrix(X_new[i, , drop = FALSE]), y = y_grid, type = "density")   data.frame(     y = pred$fit$y,     density = pred$fit$density,     label = paste0("x1=", round(X_new[i, "x1"], 1), ", x2=", X_new[i, "x2"]),     model = "Normal"   ) })  densities_cauchy <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(X_new)), function(i) {   pred <- predict(fit_sb_cauchy, x = as.matrix(X_new[i, , drop = FALSE]), y = y_grid, type = "density")   data.frame(     y = pred$fit$y,     density = pred$fit$density,     label = paste0("x1=", round(X_new[i, "x1"], 1), ", x2=", X_new[i, "x2"]),     model = "Cauchy"   ) })  df_cond <- bind_rows(densities_normal, densities_cauchy)  ggplot(df_cond, aes(x = y, y = density, color = label)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   facet_wrap(~ model) +   labs(title = "SB Conditional Predictive Densities", x = "y", y = "Density") +   theme_minimal() +   theme(legend.position = "bottom")

title: 11. Conditional DPmix with Stick-Breaking Backend
text: Stick-breaking component count fixed still supports covariate-dependent mixtures. predict(..., type = "density") returns group-specific densities X. predict(..., type = "quantile") reports posterior-mean quantiles; median 0.5 quantile shifts x1. Diagnoses rely S3 plot()/fitted() pipeline available vignettes.

title: 12. Conditional DPmixGPD with CRP Backend
text: Conditional DP mixtures allow covariate-dependent kernels. Adding GPD tail replaces bulk kernel beyond threshold, improving stability extremes. CRP backend governs allocation dynamics bulk.

code: X_new <- rbind(   c(-1, 0, 0, 0, 0),   c(0, 0, 0, 0, 0),   c(1, 1, 0, 0, 0) ) colnames(X_new) <- colnames(X) y_grid <- seq(0, max(y) * 1.2, length.out = 200)  df_pred_gamma <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(X_new)), function(i) {   pred <- predict(fit_sb_cond_gpd_gamma, x = as.matrix(X_new[i, , drop = FALSE]), y = y_grid, type = "density")   data.frame(     y = pred$fit$y,     density = pred$fit$density,     label = paste("x1=", X_new[i, 1], ", x2=", X_new[i, 2], sep = ""),     model = "Gamma"   ) })  df_pred_laplace <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(X_new)), function(i) {   pred <- predict(fit_sb_cond_gpd_laplace, x = as.matrix(X_new[i, , drop = FALSE]), y = y_grid, type = "density")   data.frame(     y = pred$fit$y,     density = pred$fit$density,     label = paste("x1=", X_new[i, 1], ", x2=", X_new[i, 2], sep = ""),     model = "Laplace"   ) })  bind_rows(df_pred_gamma, df_pred_laplace) %>%   ggplot(aes(x = y, y = density, color = label)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   facet_wrap(~ model) +   labs(title = "Conditional Density (SB + GPD)", x = "y", y = "Density") +   theme_minimal() +   theme(legend.position = "bottom")

title: 13. Conditional DPmixGPD with Stick-Breaking Backend
code: X_grid <- cbind(x1 = seq(-1, 1, length.out = 5), x2 = 0, x3 = 0, x4 = 0, x5 = 0) colnames(X_grid) <- colnames(X) quant_probs <- c(0.90, 0.95)  pred_q_gamma <- predict(fit_sb_cond_gpd_gamma, x = as.matrix(X_grid), type = "quantile", index = quant_probs) pred_q_laplace <- predict(fit_sb_cond_gpd_laplace, x = as.matrix(X_grid), type = "quantile", index = quant_probs)  quant_df_gamma <- pred_q_gamma$fit quant_df_gamma$x1 <- X_grid[quant_df_gamma$id, "x1"] quant_df_gamma$model <- "Gamma"  quant_df_laplace <- pred_q_laplace$fit quant_df_laplace$x1 <- X_grid[quant_df_laplace$id, "x1"] quant_df_laplace$model <- "Laplace"  bind_rows(quant_df_gamma, quant_df_laplace) %>%   ggplot(aes(x = x1, y = estimate, color = factor(index), group = index)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   geom_point(size = 2) +   facet_wrap(~ model) +   labs(title = "Tail Quantiles vs x1 (SB)", x = "x1", y = "Quantile", color = "Probability") +   theme_minimal()

title: 13. Conditional DPmixGPD with Stick-Breaking Backend

title: 13. Conditional DPmixGPD with Stick-Breaking Backend
text: Conditional stick-breaking mixtures capture covariate-dependent bulk structure GPD handles extremes. predict() plot() remain consistent densities, posterior-mean quantiles, residuals. Expect threshold-selected posterior-mean tail quantiles shift x1 even components fixed. Next: move causal models starting -backend CRP (v12).

what: Model B: CRP with GPD Tail (Gamma)
title: 14. Causal Inference: No X (CRP) - Gamma Kernel
code: param_specs_gpd <- list(   gpd = list(     threshold = list(       mode = "dist",       dist = "lognormal",       args = list(meanlog = log(max(u_threshold, .Machine$double.eps)), sdlog = 0.25)     )   ) )  bundle_crp_gpd <- tryCatch({   build_causal_bundle(     y = y,     T = T,     X = NULL,     kernel = "gamma",     backend = "crp",     PS = FALSE,     GPD = TRUE,     components = 6,     param_specs = param_specs_gpd,     mcmc_outcome = list(niter = 300, nburnin = 80, nchains = 1, thin = 1, seed = 2)   ) }, error = function(e) {   message("Skipping CRP + GPD example: ", conditionMessage(e))   NULL })  supports_crp_gpd <- !is.null(bundle_crp_gpd) if (supports_crp_gpd) bundle_crp_gpd if (isTRUE(supports_crp_gpd)) {   fit_crp_gpd <- quiet_mcmc(run_mcmc_causal(bundle_crp_gpd))   summary(fit_crp_gpd) } if (isTRUE(supports_crp_gpd)) {   pred_mean_gpd <- predict(fit_crp_gpd, type = "mean", interval = "credible", nsim_mean = 150)   head(pred_mean_gpd)   plot(pred_mean_gpd) } if (isTRUE(supports_crp_gpd)) {   pred_q_gpd <- predict(fit_crp_gpd, type = "quantile", p = 0.5, interval = "credible")   head(pred_q_gpd)   plot(pred_q_gpd) } if (isTRUE(supports_crp_gpd)) {   pred_d_gpd <- predict(fit_crp_gpd, y = y_eval, type = "density", interval = "credible")   head(pred_d_gpd)   plot(pred_d_gpd) } if (isTRUE(supports_crp_gpd)) {   pred_surv_gpd <- predict(fit_crp_gpd, y = y_eval, type = "survival", interval = "credible")   head(pred_surv_gpd)   plot(pred_surv_gpd) } if (isTRUE(supports_crp_gpd)) {   ate_gpd <- ate(fit_crp_gpd, interval = "credible", nsim_mean = 150)   print(ate_gpd)   summary(ate_gpd) } if (isTRUE(supports_crp_gpd)) {   ate_plots_gpd <- plot(ate_gpd)   ate_plots_gpd$treatment_effect } if (isTRUE(supports_crp_gpd)) {   qte_gpd <- qte(fit_crp_gpd, probs = c(0.25, 0.5, 0.75), interval = "credible")   print(qte_gpd)   summary(qte_gpd) } if (isTRUE(supports_crp_gpd)) {   qte_plots_gpd <- plot(qte_gpd)   qte_plots_gpd$treatment_effect }

title: 16. Causal Inference: Mixed Backends (CRP+SB) - Cauchy/Laplace
text: causal workflow fits treated control arms covariate adjustment, optionally including propensity scores. Backend choices control mixtures represented target posterior quantities.

title: 16. Causal Inference: Mixed Backends (CRP+SB) - Cauchy/Laplace
text: vignette fits two causal models using different backends kernels: Model : CRP bulk-(Cauchy) Model B: SB GPD tail (Laplace) model demonstrates full causal S3 workflow: bundle, fit, predict, plot, ATE, QTE.

title: 16. Causal Inference: Mixed Backends (CRP+SB) - Cauchy/Laplace
text: Causal Dataset Summary
code: data("causal_alt_real500_p4_k2") y <- causal_alt_real500_p4_k2$y T <- causal_alt_real500_p4_k2$T X <- as.matrix(causal_alt_real500_p4_k2$X)  summary_tbl <- tibble(   statistic = c("N", "Mean", "SD", "Min", "Max"),   value = c(length(y), mean(y), sd(y), min(y), max(y)) )  summary_tbl %>%   mutate(value = signif(value, 4)) %>%   kable(caption = "Causal Dataset Summary", align = "c") %>%   kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE, position = "center") x_eval <- X[1:40, , drop = FALSE] y_eval <- y[1:40] u_threshold <- as.numeric(stats::quantile(y, 0.8, names = FALSE)) df_causal <- data.frame(y = y, T = as.factor(T), x1 = X[, 1], x2 = X[, 2])  p_scatter <- ggplot(df_causal, aes(x = x1, y = y, color = T)) +   geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +   labs(title = "Outcome vs X1 by Treatment", x = "X1", y = "y", color = "Treatment") +   theme_minimal()  p_scatter

title: 16. Causal Inference: Mixed Backends (CRP+SB) - Cauchy/Laplace
code: bundle_crp_bulk <- build_causal_bundle(   y = y,   T = T,   X = X,   kernel = "cauchy",   backend = "crp",   PS = "logit",   GPD = FALSE,   components = 5,   mcmc_outcome = list(niter = 2500, nburnin = 500, nchains = 1, thin = 1, seed = 1),   mcmc_ps = list(niter = 2500, nburnin = 500, nchains = 1, thin = 1, seed = 1) )  bundle_crp_bulk DPmixGPD causal bundle PS model: disabled  Outcome (treated): backend = crp | kernel = cauchy  Outcome (control): backend = crp | kernel = cauchy  GPD tail (treated/control): FALSE / FALSE  components (treated/control): 5 / 5  Outcome PS included: FALSE  epsilon (treated/control): 0.025 / 0.025  n (control) = 232 | n (treated) = 268 summary(bundle_crp_bulk) DPmixGPD causal bundle summary DPmixGPD causal bundle PS model: disabled  Outcome (treated): backend = crp | kernel = cauchy  Outcome (control): backend = crp | kernel = cauchy  GPD tail (treated/control): FALSE / FALSE  components (treated/control): 5 / 5  Outcome PS included: FALSE  epsilon (treated/control): 0.025 / 0.025  n (control) = 232 | n (treated) = 268 fit_crp_bulk <- quiet_mcmc(run_mcmc_causal(bundle_crp_bulk)) summary(fit_crp_bulk) -- Outcome fits -- [control] MixGPD fit | backend: Chinese Restaurant Process | kernel: Cauchy Distribution | GPD tail: FALSE n = 232 | components = 5 | epsilon = 0.025 MCMC: niter=2500, nburnin=500, thin=1, nchains=1  Fit Use summary() for posterior summaries; plot() for diagnostics; predict() for predictions. title: 16. Causal Inference: Mixed Backends (CRP+SB) - Cauchy/Laplace

title: 18. Causal Inference: Mixed Backends (Bulk-only) - Cauchy Kernel

title: 18. Causal Inference: Mixed Backends (Bulk-only) - Cauchy Kernel
text: Outcome Summary (Cauchy)
code: data("causal_alt_real500_p4_k2") y <- causal_alt_real500_p4_k2$y T <- causal_alt_real500_p4_k2$T X <- as.matrix(causal_alt_real500_p4_k2$X)  summary_tbl <- tibble(   statistic = c("N", "Mean", "SD", "Min", "Max"),   value = c(length(y), mean(y), sd(y), min(y), max(y)) )  summary_tbl %>%   mutate(value = signif(value, 4)) %>%   kable(caption = "Outcome Summary (Cauchy)", align = "c") %>%   kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE, position = "center") x_eval <- X[1:40, , drop = FALSE] y_eval <- y[1:40] df_causal <- data.frame(y = y, T = as.factor(T), x1 = X[, 1])  p_scatter <- ggplot(df_causal, aes(x = x1, y = y, color = T)) +   geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +   labs(title = "Outcome vs X1", x = "X1", y = "y", color = "Treatment") +   theme_minimal()  p_scatter

code: bundle_crp_sb <- build_causal_bundle(   y = y,   T = T,   X = X,   kernel = c("cauchy", "cauchy"),   backend = c("crp", "sb"),   PS = "logit",   GPD = c(FALSE, FALSE),   components = c(5, 5),   mcmc_outcome = list(niter = 2500, nburnin = 500, nchains = 1, thin = 1, seed = 2),   mcmc_ps = list(niter = 2500, nburnin = 500, nchains = 1, thin = 1, seed = 2) )  bundle_crp_sb DPmixGPD causal bundle PS model: disabled  Outcome (treated): backend = crp | kernel = cauchy  Outcome (control): backend = sb | kernel = cauchy  GPD tail (treated/control): FALSE / FALSE  components (treated/control): 5 / 5  Outcome PS included: FALSE  epsilon (treated/control): 0.025 / 0.025  n (control) = 232 | n (treated) = 268 summary(bundle_crp_sb) DPmixGPD causal bundle summary DPmixGPD causal bundle PS model: disabled  Outcome (treated): backend = crp | kernel = cauchy  Outcome (control): backend = sb | kernel = cauchy  GPD tail (treated/control): FALSE / FALSE  components (treated/control): 5 / 5  Outcome PS included: FALSE  epsilon (treated/control): 0.025 / 0.025  n (control) = 232 | n (treated) = 268 fit_crp_sb <- run_mcmc_causal(bundle_crp_sb) ===== Monitors ===== thin = 1: alpha, beta_location, scale, w, z ===== Samplers ===== RW sampler (30)   - alpha   - scale[]  (5 elements)   - beta_location[]  (20 elements)   - v[]  (4 elements) categorical sampler (232)   - z[]  (232 elements) ===== Monitors ===== thin = 1: alpha, location, scale, z ===== Samplers ===== CRP_concentration sampler (1)   - alpha CRP_cluster_wrapper sampler (10)   - location[]  (5 elements)   - scale[]  (5 elements) CRP sampler (1)   - z[1:268] [Warning] CRP_sampler: This MCMC is not for a proper model. [treated] MixGPD fit | backend: Chinese Restaurant Process | kernel: Cauchy Distribution | GPD tail: FALSE n = 268 | components = 5 | epsilon = 0.025 MCMC: niter=2500, nburnin=500, thin=1, nchains=1  Fit Use summary() for posterior summaries; title: 19. Causal Inference: Mixed Backends with GPD - Amoroso Kernel
text: Mixed-backend causal models allow treatment arm use different DP mixture representation. can improve fit treated control groups exhibit distinct distributional complexity.

what: Causal Inference: Mixed Backends with GPD - Amoroso Kernel
title: 19. Causal Inference: Mixed Backends with GPD - Amoroso Kernel
text: vignette fits two mixed-backend causal models GPD tails Amoroso kernel: Model : Treated = SB, Control = CRP Model B: Treated = CRP, Control = SB shift y keep outcomes stable positive range.

title: 19. Causal Inference: Mixed Backends with GPD - Amoroso Kernel
text: Shifted Outcome Summary (Amoroso)
code: data("causal_alt_real500_p4_k2") y_raw <- causal_alt_real500_p4_k2$y T <- causal_alt_real500_p4_k2$T X <- as.matrix(causal_alt_real500_p4_k2$X)  shift <- abs(min(y_raw)) + 0.1 y <- y_raw + shift  summary_tbl <- tibble(   statistic = c("N", "Mean", "SD", "Min", "Max"),   value = c(length(y), mean(y), sd(y), min(y), max(y)) )  summary_tbl %>%   mutate(value = signif(value, 4)) %>%   kable(caption = "Shifted Outcome Summary (Amoroso)", align = "c") %>%   kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE, position = "center") x_eval <- X[1:40, , drop = FALSE] y_eval <- y[1:40] u_threshold <- as.numeric(stats::quantile(y, 0.8, names = FALSE)) df_causal <- data.frame(y = y, T = as.factor(T), x1 = X[, 1])  p_scatter <- ggplot(df_causal, aes(x = x1, y = y, color = T)) +   geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +   labs(title = "Shifted Outcome vs X1", x = "X1", y = "y (shifted)", color = "Treatment") +   theme_minimal()  p_scatter

title: 20. Troubleshooting
text: troubleshooting issues relate model compilation, MCMC stability, data compatibility. Understanding DP mixture structure MCMC diagnostics helps identify whether issues stem model specification, priors, runtime limits.

title: 20. Troubleshooting
text: vignette collects common errors fixes encountered building running DPmixGPD models.

what: 1. “Number of columns in ‘x’ does not match training design matrix”
title: 20. Troubleshooting
text: Cause: New data different columns training X. Fix: Use column names order training matrix.
code: X_train <- fit$data$X X_new <- X_train[1:5, , drop = FALSE] X_new <- X_new[, colnames(X_train), drop = FALSE]  predict(fit, x = X_new, type = "mean")

what: 2. “Cauchy kernels are never paired with GPD tails”
title: 20. Troubleshooting
text: Cause: Cauchy kernel allowed GPD tails. Fix: Use different kernel disable GPD.
code: bundle <- build_nimble_bundle(   y = y,   kernel = "cauchy",   backend = "sb",   GPD = FALSE )

title: 20. Troubleshooting
text: Cause: Observational causal modeling requires covariates PS estimation. Fix: Provide X, switch design = "rct".
code: cb <- build_causal_bundle(   y = y,   T = T,   X = X,   design = "observational",   PS = "logit" )

title: 20. Troubleshooting
text: Cause: short chains overly restrictive priors. Fix: Increase iterations relax tail priors.
code: bundle <- build_nimble_bundle(   y = y,   kernel = "gamma",   backend = "crp",   GPD = TRUE,   components = 6,   mcmc = mcmc )

title: 20. Troubleshooting
text: Cause: Parallel predictions export large functions/objects. Fix: Set higher limit use ncores = 1.
code: options(future.globals.maxSize = 4 * 1024^3)  pred <- predict(fit, x = X_new, y = y_grid, type = "density", ncores = 2)

title: 20. Troubleshooting
text: Cause: Large datasets, many components, heavy kernels. Fix: Use smaller components/J, shorter niter, start CRP.
code: bundle <- build_nimble_bundle(   y = y,   backend = "crp",   kernel = "normal",   components = 4,   mcmc = mcmc )

what: 7. Prediction returns constant fitted values in unconditional models
title: 20. Troubleshooting
text: Cause: Unconditional fitted values model-based population summaries. Fix: expected. Use conditional models need observation-specific fitted values.
code: fit_vals <- fitted(fit) head(fit_vals)

what: 8. PS not used in causal model
title: 20. Troubleshooting
text: Cause: PS=FALSE X missing. Fix: Provide X enable PS.
code: cb <- build_causal_bundle(   y = y,   T = T,   X = X,   PS = "logit" ) fit <- run_mcmc_causal(cb)

title: 20. Troubleshooting
text: Cause: Using kernels different supports across arms. Fix: Ensure arms use kernels support (e.g., positive-support real-line).
code: cb <- build_causal_bundle(   y = y,   T = T,   X = X,   kernel = c("gamma", "lognormal"),   backend = c("sb", "crp") )

title: MCMC Workflow
text: posterior explored Markov chain Monte Carlo. Diagnostics summarize mixing convergence behavior, traceplots, effective sample size, Geweke statistics comparing early vs late chain segments. checks help ensure posterior summaries reliable.

title: Model Specification
text: vignette describes probabilistic model behind DPmixGPD user inputs map compiled NIMBLE bundle.

title: Model Specification
text: outcomes yiy_i kernel KK, bulk distribution modeled DP mixture: $$ f(y_i) = \\int K(y_i; \\theta)\\, dG(\\theta), \\quad G \\sim \\mathrm{DP}(\\alpha, G_0). $$ DP prior GG induces clustering component parameters allows mixture adapt unknown distributional shapes.

title: Model Specification
text: $\\mathrm{GPD} = \\mathrm{TRUE}$, Generalized Pareto tail replaces bulk kernel beyond threshold uu: $$ f(y) = \\begin{cases} f_{\\text{bulk}}(y), & y \\le u \\\\ f_{\\text{GPD}}(y; u, \\sigma, \\xi), & y > u \\end{cases} $$ continuity enforced threshold.

title: Model Specification
text: Conditional models allow kernel parameters depend covariates xix_i: $$ f(y_i \\mid x_i) = \\int K(y_i; \\theta(x_i))\\, dG(\\theta). $$ linking structure kernel-specific implemented model builder.

title: Model Specification
text: workflows begin build_nimble_bundle(). bundle includes compiled NIMBLE code MCMC settings.
code: library(DPmixGPD)  data("faithful", package = "datasets") y <- faithful$eruptions  bundle_uncond <- build_nimble_bundle(   y = y,   backend = "sb",   kernel  = "normal",   GPD     = TRUE,   components = 6,   mcmc = list(niter = 200, nburnin = 50, thin = 2, nchains = 1, seed = 1) )

title: Model Specification
code: data("mtcars", package = "datasets") df <- mtcars X <- df[, c("wt", "hp")] X <- as.data.frame(X) y <- df$mpg  bundle_cond <- build_nimble_bundle(   y = y,   X = X,   backend = "sb",   kernel  = "normal",   GPD     = TRUE,   components = 6,   mcmc = list(niter = 200, nburnin = 50, thin = 2, nchains = 1, seed = 1) )

title: Model Specification
text: Model Specification Arguments

title: Model Specification
text: conditional models, length(y) must equal nrow(X) Covariates must numeric regression-style links Covariate names must use NIMBLE reserved keywords (, , , etc.)
code: # Rename reserved keywords names(X)[names(X) == "if"] <- "x_if"

title: Unconditional Models
text: vignette demonstrates fitting unconditional DP mixture model optional GPD tail augmentation.

title: Unconditional Models
text: Unconditional models treat observations exchangeable draws mixture distribution. DP prior mixing measure GG yields flexible density estimation: $$ y_i \\mid G \\sim \\int K(y_i; \\theta)\\, dG(\\theta), \\quad G \\sim \\mathrm{DP}(\\alpha, G_0). $$ $\\mathrm{GPD} = \\mathrm{TRUE}$, GPD tail replaces bulk kernel beyond threshold uu stabilize tail behavior.

title: Unconditional Models
code: library(DPmixGPD)  data("faithful", package = "datasets") y <- faithful$eruptions  bundle <- build_nimble_bundle(   y = y,   backend = "sb",   kernel = "normal",   GPD = TRUE,   components = 6,   mcmc = mcmc )  fit <- run_mcmc_bundle_manual(bundle, show_progress = FALSE)

title: Unconditional Models
code: pred_mean <- predict(fit, type = "mean", cred.level = 0.90, interval = "credible") pred_q95  <- predict(fit, type = "quantile", index = 0.95, cred.level = 0.90, interval = "credible")  pred_mean$fit #>   estimate lower upper #> 1     3.19  3.05  3.32 pred_q95$fit #>   estimate index lower upper #> 1     4.81  0.95  4.72  4.93

title: Amoroso distribution — amoroso
text: Base Amoroso distribution functions implemented package. Function names parameterization follow existing Amoroso implementation. d*, p*, q* functions accept vector inputs first argument evaluate elementwise; r* supports n > 1.

code: dAmoroso(x, loc, scale, shape1, shape2, log = 0)  pAmoroso(q, loc, scale, shape1, shape2, lower.tail = 1, log.p = 0)  qAmoroso(p, loc, scale, shape1, shape2, lower.tail = TRUE, log.p = FALSE)  rAmoroso(n, loc, scale, shape1, shape2)

title: Amoroso distribution — amoroso
text: x Numeric scalar giving point density evaluated. loc Numeric scalar location parameter. scale Numeric scalar scale parameter. shape1 Numeric scalar first shape parameter. shape2 Numeric scalar second shape parameter. log Logical; TRUE, return log-density (integer flag 0/1 NIMBLE). q Numeric scalar giving point distribution function evaluated. lower.tail Logical; TRUE (default), probabilities \(P(X \le q)\). log.p Logical; TRUE, probabilities returned log scale. p Numeric scalar probability \((0,1)\) quantile function. n Integer giving number draws. portability inside NIMBLE, RNG implementation supports n = 1.

what: Amoroso mixture with a GPD tail — amoroso_mixgpd
title: Amoroso mixture with a GPD tail — amoroso_mixgpd
text: family splices generalized Pareto distribution (GPD) threshold threshold onto Amoroso mixture bulk. Let \(F_{mix}\) denote Amoroso mixture CDF. spliced CDF \(F(x)=F_{mix}(x)\) \(x<threshold\) \(F(x)=F_{mix}(threshold) + \left\{1-F_{mix}(threshold)\right\}G(x)\) \(x\ge threshold\), \(G\) GPD CDF exceedances threshold.

title: Build a causal bundle (design + two outcome arms) — build_causal_bundle
text: y Numeric outcome vector. X Design matrix data.frame covariates (N x P). T Binary treatment indicator (length N, values 0/1). backend Character; "sb" "crp" outcome models. length 2, first entry used treated (T=1) second control (T=0). kernel Character kernel name outcome models. length 2, first entry used treated (T=1) second control (T=0). GPD Logical; include GPD tail outcomes TRUE. length 2, first entry used treated (T=1) second control (T=0). components Deprecated alias J. one J components supplied. J Integer >= 2; truncation parameter outcome mixtures. length 2, first entry used treated (T=1) second control (T=0). param_specs Outcome parameter overrides (structure build_nimble_bundle()). can pass single list used arms list con trt entries. mcmc_outcome MCMC settings list outcome bundles. mcmc_ps MCMC settings list PS model. epsilon Numeric [0,1) used outcome bundles posterior truncation summaries. length 2, first entry used treated (T=1) second control (T=0). alpha_random Logical; whether outcome concentration alpha stochastic. ps_model PS model specification backward compatibility (deprecated). Recommend using PS parameter instead. ps_prior Normal prior PS coefficients. List mean sd. include_intercept Logical; TRUE, intercept column prepended X PS model. PS Character logical; controls propensity score estimation: "logit" (default): Logistic regression PS model "probit": Probit regression PS model "naive": Gaussian naive Bayes PS model FALSE: PS estimation; outcome models use X PS model choice stored bundle metadata downstream use prediction summaries, enabling seamless integration future PS estimation methods. design Causal design: "rct" "observational". ps_scale Scale used augmenting outcomes PS: "logit" "prob". ps_summary Posterior summary PS: "mean" "median". ps_clamp Numeric epsilon clamping PS values \((\epsilon, 1-\epsilon)\).

title: Build a causal bundle (design + two outcome arms) — build_causal_bundle
text: list class "dpmixgpd_causal_bundle".

title: Build a causal bundle (design + two outcome arms) — build_causal_bundle
text: outcome bundles reuse existing DPM + optional GPD tail machinery. PS model lightweight NIMBLE estimator supporting logit/probit regression naive Bayes classifier simple priors.

title: Build a causal bundle (design + two outcome arms) — build_causal_bundle
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ set.seed(1) N <- 100 X <- cbind(x1 = rnorm(N), x2 = runif(N)) T <- rbinom(N, 1, plogis(0.3 + 0.5 * X[, 1])) y <- rexp(N) + 0.1  cb <- build_causal_bundle(   y = y,   X = X,   T = T,   backend = "sb",   kernel = "gamma",   GPD = TRUE,   J = 10,   PS = "probit" ) } # }

title: Build a NIMBLE bundle — build_nimble_bundle
text: Creates runnable "bundle" containing: compiled model spec nimbleCode model code constants, data, explicit dimensions initialization function inits (stored function) monitor specification MCMC settings list (stored used code generation)

title: Build a NIMBLE bundle — build_nimble_bundle
code: build_nimble_bundle(   y,   X = NULL,   ps = NULL,   backend = c("sb", "crp"),   kernel,   GPD = FALSE,   components = NULL,   J = NULL,   param_specs = NULL,   mcmc = list(niter = 2000, nburnin = 500, thin = 1, nchains = 1, seed = 1),   epsilon = 0.025,   alpha_random = TRUE )

title: Build a NIMBLE bundle — build_nimble_bundle
text: y Numeric outcome vector. X Optional design matrix/data.frame (N x p) conditional variants. ps Optional numeric vector (length N) propensity scores. provided, augments design matrix PS-adjusted outcome modeling. backend Character; "sb" (stick-breaking) "crp" (Chinese Restaurant Process). kernel Character kernel name (must exist get_kernel_registry()). GPD Logical; whether GPD tail requested. components Deprecated alias J. one J components supplied. J Integer >= 2. Single user-facing truncation parameter: SB: number mixture components used stick-breaking truncation CRP: maximum number clusters represented finite NIMBLE model param_specs Optional list entries bulk tail override defaults. mcmc Named list MCMC settings (niter, nburnin, thin, nchains, seed). Stored bundle. epsilon Numeric [0,1). downstream summaries/plots/prediction keep smaller k defined either () cumulative mass >= 1 - epsilon (ii) per-component weights >= epsilon, renormalize. alpha_random Logical; whether concentration alpha stochastic.

title: Build a NIMBLE bundle — build_nimble_bundle
text: named list (bundle) class "dpmixgpd_bundle".

title: Build a NIMBLE bundle — build_nimble_bundle
text: function intentionally stops "pre-run" stage (spec/code/constants/data/dimensions/inits/monitors). Use run_mcmc_bundle_manual() execute MCMC stored settings.

title: Build a NIMBLE bundle — build_nimble_bundle
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ y <- abs(rnorm(60)) + 0.1 bundle <- build_nimble_bundle(   y = y,   backend = "sb",   kernel = "normal",   GPD = FALSE,   components = 4,   mcmc = list(niter = 200, nburnin = 50, thin = 1, nchains = 1, seed = 1) ) bundle } # }

title: Cauchy distribution — cauchy
text: Base Cauchy distribution functions implemented nimbleFunctions can used NIMBLE models. Parameterization uses location scale (scale > 0). d*, p*, q* functions accept vector inputs first argument evaluate elementwise; r* supports n > 1.

title: Cauchy distribution — cauchy
code: dCauchy(x, location, scale, log = 0)  pCauchy(q, location, scale, lower.tail = 1, log.p = 0)  rCauchy(n, location, scale)  qCauchy(p, location, scale, lower.tail = TRUE, log.p = FALSE)

title: Cauchy distribution — cauchy
text: x Numeric scalar giving point density evaluated. location Numeric scalar location parameter. scale Numeric scalar scale parameter; must positive. log Logical; TRUE, return log-density (integer flag 0/1 NIMBLE). q Numeric scalar giving point distribution function evaluated. lower.tail Logical; TRUE (default), probabilities \(P(X \le q)\). log.p Logical; TRUE, probabilities returned log scale. n Integer giving number draws. portability inside NIMBLE, RNG implementation supports n = 1. p Numeric scalar probability \((0,1)\) quantile function.

title: Cauchy distribution — cauchy
text: dCauchy returns numeric scalar density; pCauchy returns numeric scalar CDF; rCauchy returns one random draw; qCauchy returns numeric quantile.

title: Cauchy distribution — cauchy
text: dCauchy(): Cauchy density function pCauchy(): Cauchy distribution function rCauchy(): Cauchy random generation qCauchy(): Cauchy quantile function

title: Cauchy distribution — cauchy
code: location <- 0 scale <- 1.5  dCauchy(0.5, location, scale, log = 0) #> [1] 0.191 pCauchy(0.5, location, scale, lower.tail = 1, log.p = 0) #> [1] 0.602 qCauchy(0.50, location, scale) #> [1] 0 qCauchy(0.95, location, scale) #> [1] 9.47 replicate(10, rCauchy(1, location, scale)) #>  [1]   1.00  -1.55 -10.63   5.15  -6.64 153.59   0.55  -2.49   5.13 -12.69

title: Cauchy mixture distribution — cauchy_mix
text: finite mixture Cauchy components. Base Cauchy functions taken stats. mixture density distribution function computed weighted sums. Random generation samples component index according weights draws corresponding component. Quantiles computed numerical inversion mixture CDF. d*, p*, q* functions accept vector inputs first argument evaluate elementwise; r* supports n > 1.

title: Cauchy mixture distribution — cauchy_mix
code: dCauchyMix(x, w, location, scale, log = 0)  pCauchyMix(q, w, location, scale, lower.tail = 1, log.p = 0)  rCauchyMix(n, w, location, scale)  qCauchyMix(   p,   w,   location,   scale,   lower.tail = TRUE,   log.p = FALSE,   tol = 1e-10,   maxiter = 200 )

title: Cauchy mixture distribution — cauchy_mix
text: x Numeric scalar giving point density evaluated. w Numeric vector mixture weights length \(K\). functions normalize w internally needed. location, scale Numeric vectors length \(K\) giving component locations scales. log Logical; TRUE, return log-density. q Numeric scalar giving point distribution function evaluated. lower.tail Logical; TRUE (default), probabilities \(P(X \le q)\). log.p Logical; TRUE, probabilities returned log scale. n Integer giving number draws. RNG implementation supports n = 1. p Numeric scalar probability \((0,1)\) quantile function. tol Numeric scalar tolerance passed stats::uniroot. maxiter Integer maximum number iterations stats::uniroot.

title: Cauchy mixture distribution — cauchy_mix
text: Density/CDF/RNG functions return numeric scalars. qCauchyMix returns numeric vector length p.

title: Cauchy mixture distribution — cauchy_mix
text: dCauchyMix(): Cauchy mixture density pCauchyMix(): Cauchy mixture distribution function rCauchyMix(): Cauchy mixture random generation qCauchyMix(): Cauchy mixture quantile function

title: Cauchy mixture distribution — cauchy_mix
code: w <- c(0.50, 0.30, 0.20) location <- c(-2, 0, 3) scale <- c(1.0, 0.7, 1.5)  dCauchyMix(0.5, w = w, location = location, scale = scale, log = FALSE) #> [1] 0.124 pCauchyMix(0.5, w = w, location = location, scale = scale,            lower.tail = TRUE, log.p = FALSE) #> [1] 0.683 qCauchyMix(0.50, w = w, location = location, scale = scale) #> [1] -0.664 qCauchyMix(0.95, w = w, location = location, scale = scale) #> [1] 7 replicate(10, rCauchyMix(1, w = w, location = location, scale = scale)) #>  [1]  5.345 -0.846 -0.862 -2.580 -0.690  1.779  5.349  3.392 45.684 -0.874

title: Lowercase vectorized Cauchy mixture distribution functions — cauchy_mix_lowercase

what: Gamma with a GPD tail — gamma_gpd
title: Gamma with a GPD tail — gamma_gpd
text: Splices generalized Pareto distribution (GPD) threshold onto single Gamma bulk distribution parameterized scale scale shape shape. bulk probability threshold, \(F_{bulk}(threshold)\), scales tail mass overall CDF proper.

title: Gamma with a GPD tail — gamma_gpd
code: dGammaGpd(x, shape, scale, threshold, tail_scale, tail_shape, log = 0)  pGammaGpd(   q,   shape,   scale,   threshold,   tail_scale,   tail_shape,   lower.tail = 1,   log.p = 0 )  rGammaGpd(n, shape, scale, threshold, tail_scale, tail_shape)  qGammaGpd(   p,   shape,   scale,   threshold,   tail_scale,   tail_shape,   lower.tail = TRUE,   log.p = FALSE,   tol = 1e-10,   maxiter = 200 )

title: Gamma with a GPD tail — gamma_gpd
text: x Numeric scalar giving point density evaluated. shape Numeric scalar Gamma shape parameter. scale Numeric scalar scale parameter Gamma bulk. threshold Numeric scalar threshold GPD tail attached. tail_scale Numeric scalar GPD scale parameter; must positive. tail_shape Numeric scalar GPD shape parameter. log Integer flag 0/1; 1, return log-density. q Numeric scalar giving point distribution function evaluated. lower.tail Integer flag 0/1; 1 (default), probabilities \(P(X \le q)\). log.p Integer flag 0/1; 1, probabilities returned log scale. n Integer giving number draws. what: Lognormal mixture with a Gpd tail — lognormal_mixgpd
title: Lognormal mixture with a Gpd tail — lognormal_mixgpd
text: Splices generalized Pareto distribution (Gpd) threshold onto Lognormal mixture bulk. Let \(F_{mix}\) Lognormal mixture CDF. spliced CDF \(F(x)=F_{mix}(x)\) \(x<threshold\) \(F(x)=F_{mix}(threshold) + \{1-F_{mix}(threshold)\}G(x)\) \(x\ge threshold\), \(G\) Gpd CDF exceedances threshold.

title: Lognormal mixture with a Gpd tail — lognormal_mixgpd
code: dLognormalMixGpd(   x,   w,   meanlog,   sdlog,   threshold,   tail_scale,   tail_shape,   log = 0 )  pLognormalMixGpd(   q,   w,   meanlog,   sdlog,   threshold,   tail_scale,   tail_shape,   lower.tail = 1,   log.p = 0 )  rLognormalMixGpd(n, w, meanlog, sdlog, threshold, tail_scale, tail_shape)  qLognormalMixGpd(   p,   w,   meanlog,   sdlog,   threshold,   tail_scale,   tail_shape,   lower.tail = TRUE,   log.p = FALSE,   tol = 1e-10,   maxiter = 200 )

title: Lognormal mixture with a Gpd tail — lognormal_mixgpd
text: x Numeric scalar giving point density evaluated. w Numeric vector mixture weights length \(K\). meanlog, sdlog Numeric vectors length \(K\) giving component log-means log-standard deviations. threshold Numeric scalar threshold Gpd tail attached. tail_scale Numeric scalar Gpd scale parameter; must positive. tail_shape Numeric scalar Gpd shape parameter. log Integer flag 0/1; 1, return log-density. q Numeric scalar giving point distribution function evaluated. lower.tail Integer flag 0/1; 1 (default), probabilities \(P(X \le q)\). log.p Integer flag 0/1; 1, probabilities returned log scale. n Integer giving number draws. RNG implementation supports n = 1. p Numeric scalar probability \((0,1)\) quantile function. tol Numeric scalar tolerance passed stats::uniroot quantile inversion. maxiter Integer maximum number iterations stats::uniroot.

title: Lognormal mixture with a Gpd tail — lognormal_mixgpd
text: Spliced density/CDF/RNG functions return numeric scalars. qLognormalMixGpd returns numeric vector length p.

title: Lognormal mixture with a Gpd tail — lognormal_mixgpd
text: density, CDF, RNG implemented nimbleFunctions. quantile R function: uses numerical inversion bulk region closed-form Gpd quantile tail.

title: Lognormal mixture with a Gpd tail — lognormal_mixgpd
text: dLognormalMixGpd(): Lognormal mixture + Gpd tail density pLognormalMixGpd(): Lognormal mixture + Gpd tail distribution function rLognormalMixGpd(): Lognormal mixture + Gpd tail random generation qLognormalMixGpd(): Lognormal mixture + Gpd tail quantile function

title: Lognormal mixture with a Gpd tail — lognormal_mixgpd
code: w <- c(0.60, 0.25, 0.15) meanlog <- c(-0.2, 0.6, 1.2) sdlog <- c(0.4, 0.3, 0.5) threshold <- 3 tail_scale <- 0.9 tail_shape <- 0.2  dLognormalMixGpd(4.0, w = w, meanlog = meanlog, sdlog = sdlog,                 threshold = threshold, tail_scale = tail_scale,                 tail_shape = tail_shape, log = FALSE) #> [1] 0.0332 pLognormalMixGpd(4.0, w = w, meanlog = meanlog, sdlog = sdlog,                 threshold = threshold, tail_scale = tail_scale,                 tail_shape = tail_shape, lower.tail = TRUE, log.p = FALSE) #> [1] 0.964 qLognormalMixGpd(0.50, w = w, meanlog = meanlog, sdlog = sdlog,                 threshold = threshold, tail_scale = tail_scale,                 tail_shape = tail_shape) #> [1] 1.15 qLognormalMixGpd(0.95, w = w, meanlog = meanlog, sdlog = sdlog,                 threshold = threshold, tail_scale = tail_scale,                 tail_shape = tail_shape) #> [1] 3.66 replicate(10, rLognormalMixGpd(1, w = w, meanlog = meanlog, sdlog = sdlog,                               threshold = threshold,                               tail_scale = tail_scale,                               tail_shape = tail_shape)) #>  [1] 1.133 3.115 1.213 1.420 0.764 1.478 0.554 5.907 0.678 2.633
