what: Lowercase vectorized Normal distribution functions — normal_lowercase
title: Lowercase vectorized Normal distribution functions — normal_lowercase
text: Vectorized R wrappers Normal mixture, Normal mixture + GPD, Normal + GPD distribution functions. lowercase versions accept vector inputs first argument (x, q, p) return numeric vector. r* functions support n > 1.

title: Lowercase vectorized Normal distribution functions — normal_lowercase
code: dnormmix(x, w, mean, sd, log = FALSE)  pnormmix(q, w, mean, sd, lower.tail = TRUE, log.p = FALSE)  qnormmix(   p,   w,   mean,   sd,   lower.tail = TRUE,   log.p = FALSE,   tol = 1e-10,   maxiter = 200 )  rnormmix(n, w, mean, sd)  dnormmixgpd(x, w, mean, sd, threshold, tail_scale, tail_shape, log = FALSE)  pnormmixgpd(   q,   w,   mean,   sd,   threshold,   tail_scale,   tail_shape,   lower.tail = TRUE,   log.p = FALSE )  qnormmixgpd(   p,   w,   mean,   sd,   threshold,   tail_scale,   tail_shape,   lower.tail = TRUE,   log.p = FALSE,   tol = 1e-10,   maxiter = 200 )  rnormmixgpd(n, w, mean, sd, threshold, tail_scale, tail_shape)  dnormgpd(x, mean, sd, threshold, tail_scale, tail_shape, log = FALSE)  pnormgpd(   q,   mean,   sd,   threshold,   tail_scale,   tail_shape,   lower.tail = TRUE,   log.p = FALSE )  qnormgpd(   p,   mean,   sd,   threshold,   tail_scale,   tail_shape,   lower.tail = TRUE,   log.p = FALSE )  rnormgpd(n, mean, sd, threshold, tail_scale, tail_shape)

title: Lowercase vectorized Normal distribution functions — normal_lowercase
text: x Numeric vector quantiles. w Numeric vector mixture weights. mean, sd Numeric vectors (mix) scalars (base+gpd) component parameters. log Logical; TRUE, return log-density. q Numeric vector quantiles. lower.tail Logical; TRUE (default), probabilities \(P(X \le x)\). log.p Logical; TRUE, probabilities log scale. p Numeric vector probabilities. tol, maxiter Tolerance max iterations numerical inversion. n Integer number observations generate. threshold, tail_scale, tail_shape GPD tail parameters (scalars).

title: Lowercase vectorized Normal distribution functions — normal_lowercase
text: Numeric vector densities, probabilities, quantiles, random variates.

title: Lowercase vectorized Normal distribution functions — normal_lowercase
text: dnormmix(): Normal mixture density (vectorized) pnormmix(): Normal mixture distribution function (vectorized) qnormmix(): Normal mixture quantile function (vectorized) rnormmix(): Normal mixture random generation (vectorized) dnormmixgpd(): Normal mixture + GPD density (vectorized) pnormmixgpd(): Normal mixture + GPD distribution function (vectorized) qnormmixgpd(): Normal mixture + GPD quantile function (vectorized) rnormmixgpd(): Normal mixture + GPD random generation (vectorized) dnormgpd(): Normal + GPD density (vectorized) pnormgpd(): Normal + GPD distribution function (vectorized) qnormgpd(): Normal + GPD quantile function (vectorized) rnormgpd(): Normal + GPD random generation (vectorized)

title: Lowercase vectorized Normal distribution functions — normal_lowercase
code: w <- c(0.6, 0.25, 0.15) mu <- c(-1, 0.5, 2) sig <- c(1, 0.7, 1.3)  # Normal mixture dnormmix(c(0, 1, 2), w = w, mean = mu, sd = sig) #> [1] 0.270 0.177 0.063 rnormmix(5, w = w, mean = mu, sd = sig) #> [1] -2.18  1.50 -1.61 -3.81 -1.66  # Normal mixture + GPD dnormmixgpd(c(1, 2, 3), w = w, mean = mu, sd = sig,             threshold = 2, tail_scale = 1, tail_shape = 0.2) #> [1] 0.1770 0.0798 0.0267  # Normal + GPD (single component) dnormgpd(c(1, 2, 3), mean = 0.5, sd = 1, threshold = 2,          tail_scale = 1, tail_shape = 0.2) #> [1] 0.3521 0.0668 0.0224

title: Normal mixture distribution — normal_mix
text: finite mixture Normal components. Base Normal functions taken stats. Mixture density CDF computed weighted sums. Random generation samples component index according weights draws corresponding component. Quantiles computed numerical inversion mixture CDF. d*, p*, q* functions accept vector inputs first argument evaluate elementwise; r* supports n > 1.

what: Predict from a causal fit — predict.dpmixgpd_causal_fit
title: Predict from a causal fit — predict.dpmixgpd_causal_fit
text: Provides unified interface treated control outcome models guaranteeing propensity scores used arms. new covariates, PS model stored object$ps_fit used estimate required scores unless user supplies via ps. bundle built PS=FALSE, PS model exist outcome predictions use original covariates X.

title: Predict from a causal fit — predict.dpmixgpd_causal_fit
code: # S3 method for class 'dpmixgpd_causal_fit' predict(   object,   x = NULL,   y = NULL,   ps = NULL,   newdata = NULL,   type = c("mean", "quantile", "density", "survival", "prob"),   p = NULL,   nsim = NULL,   interval = "credible",   probs = c(0.025, 0.5, 0.975),   store_draws = TRUE,   nsim_mean = 200L,   ncores = 1L,   ... )

title: Predict from a causal fit — predict.dpmixgpd_causal_fit
text: object "dpmixgpd_causal_fit" object returned run_mcmc_causal(). x Optional new data. Alias newdata. y Numeric vector evaluation points (required type="density" "survival"). ps Optional numeric vector propensity scores aligned x / newdata. provided, supplied scores used instead recomputing stored PS model (needed custom inputs). newdata Optional new data. NULL, uses training design (stored). type Prediction type. Supported: "mean", "quantile", "density", "survival", "prob". p Numeric vector probabilities quantiles (required type="quantile"). nsim Number posterior predictive samples (type="sample"). interval Character NULL; type credible interval: NULL interval, "credible" equal-tailed quantile intervals (default), "hpd" highest posterior density intervals. probs Quantiles credible interval bands. store_draws Logical; whether store posterior draws (type="sample"). nsim_mean Number posterior predictive samples use posterior mean estimation (type="mean"). ncores Number CPU cores use parallel prediction (supported). ... Unused.

title: Predict from a causal fit — predict.dpmixgpd_causal_fit
text: "mean" "quantile", numeric matrix columns ps, estimate, lower, upper, representing treated-minus-control posterior summaries. PS disabled X absent, ps NA PS used. "density", "survival", "prob", data frame columns y, ps, trt_estimate, trt_lower, trt_upper, con_estimate, con_lower, con_upper.

title: Predict from a causal fit — predict.dpmixgpd_causal_fit
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ cb <- build_causal_bundle(y = y, X = X, T = T, backend = "sb", kernel = "normal") fit <- run_mcmc_causal(cb) predict(fit, x = X[1:10, ], type = "quantile", p = c(0.25, 0.5, 0.75)) predict(fit, x = X[1:10, ], type = "mean", interval = "hpd")  # HPD intervals predict(fit, x = X[1:10, ], type = "mean", interval = NULL)   # No intervals } # }

title: Predict from a MixGPD fit — predict.mixgpd_fit
text: object stores dedicated predictive machinery, can keep signature swap internals without breaking user code.

title: Predict from a MixGPD fit — predict.mixgpd_fit
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ y <- abs(stats::rnorm(50)) + 0.1 bundle <- build_nimble_bundle(y = y, backend = "sb", kernel = "normal",                              GPD = TRUE, components = 6,                              mcmc = list(niter = 200, nburnin = 50, thin = 1, nchains = 1)) fit <- run_mcmc_bundle_manual(bundle) pr <- predict(fit, type = "quantile", p = c(0.5, 0.9)) pr_surv <- predict(fit, y = sort(y), type = "survival") pr_cdf <- list(fit = 1 - pr_surv$fit) # HPD intervals pr_hpd <- predict(fit, type = "quantile", p = c(0.5, 0.9), interval = "hpd") # No intervals pr_none <- predict(fit, type = "quantile", p = c(0.5, 0.9), interval = NULL) } # }

title: Summarize a QTE object — summary.dpmixgpd_qte
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ cb <- build_causal_bundle(y = y, X = X, T = T, backend = "sb", kernel = "normal", J = 6) fit <- run_mcmc_causal(cb, show_progress = FALSE) q <- qte(fit, probs = c(0.25, 0.5, 0.75), interval = "credible") summary(q) } # }

title: Summarize a MixGPD fitted object — summary.mixgpd_fit
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ y <- abs(stats::rnorm(50)) + 0.1 bundle <- build_nimble_bundle(y = y, backend = "sb", kernel = "normal",                              GPD = TRUE, components = 6,                              mcmc = list(niter = 200, nburnin = 50, thin = 1, nchains = 1)) fit <- run_mcmc_bundle_manual(bundle) summary(fit, pars = c("alpha", "threshold")) } # }
